prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a hask has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the hask-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a hask, a prit, and a skiv. The hask is underneath the prit. The prit is to-the-left-of the skiv. The hask has a green button on top, a yellow button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The prit has a yellow button on top and a green button on bottom. The skiv has a blue button on top and a green button on side. Pressing the green button on the hask activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a pell, a drog, and a glim. The glim is underneath the pell. The pell is to-the-left-of the drog. The pell has a yellow button on top and a green button on side. The drog has a blue button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The glim has a green button on top, a yellow button on side, and a blue button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the hask-analog, and which button activates it?
